symbols: crown on Ursula's head — royal status and heavenly reward of the faithful martyr
palm frond held by angel — universal symbol of martyrdom and victory over death
floral wreath offered by cherub — heavenly honor and the beauty of sanctity
golden mantle — regal dignity and the glory bestowed upon the saints
companion virgins behind her — the legendary eleven thousand who followed Ursula in faith and martyrdom
dark background — the world's hostility through which the saints shine as lights
flowers at her feet — the blossoming of virtue and the paradise that awaits the faithful
description: A tall, regal female figure dominates the center of the composition, wearing an elaborate golden mantle over a white and rose-colored dress, with a gold crown upon her head. She gazes downward with composed serenity. Two winged cherubs hover above: one offers a floral wreath, the other holds a large green palm frond. Flanking the central figure at lower height are groups of smaller figures — women and elderly persons — some holding staffs or palms. The background is very dark, making the richly colored garments and luminous skin of the figures stand out dramatically.

scripture_refs.reason: The great multitude in white robes holding palm branches, having come through great tribulation, parallels the company of virgin martyrs led by Ursula
The 144,000 who are virgins and follow the Lamb wherever he goes resonates with the tradition of Ursula's virgin companions
The crown of righteousness awaiting those who have fought the good fight, visually realized in Ursula's royal crown
The parable of the wise virgins who keep their lamps ready prefigures the faithful virgin saints who persevere unto death
